events.headline: Lutheran Kallstadt Shapes the Trump Line
events.body: Friedrich Trump is baptized in the village church of Kallstadt, a Rhenish Palatinate town whose faith runs Lutheran to the bone. The pietist work-ethic and prosperity theology latent in German Lutheranism travel with the family when they cross the Atlantic.

events.headline: Carnegie Declares Wealth a Divine Trust
events.body: Andrew Carnegie's essay 'The Gospel of Wealth' frames the Gilded Age tycoon as God's appointed steward, obligated to accumulate and then dispense fortune on behalf of the less worthy. The ideology of laissez-faire capitalism acquires a Protestant halo.

events.headline: Walter Rauschenbusch Preaches the Social Gospel
events.body: The Baptist minister from Hell's Kitchen argues that sin is structural and salvation is social, launching a reformist theology that pushes Protestant churches toward labor rights, poverty relief, and civic activism against Gilded Age excess.

events.headline: The Fundamentals Draws Battle Lines in Protestantism
events.body: A series of pamphlets called 'The Fundamentals' announces that biblical inerrancy, the virgin birth, and the bodily resurrection are non-negotiable — sparking a fifty-year war between modernist and fundamentalist wings of American Protestantism that will reshape the country's politics.

events.headline: Lutheran Confessionalism Yields Ground to the Revivalists
events.body: As the Billy Sunday revival circuit sweeps through the Northeast, the ordered liturgical tradition of German Lutheranism — which the Trump family's heritage touches — loses the battle for working-class Protestant identity to emotional, anti-intellectual Baptist and Pentecostal strains. An alternate history in which immigrant Lutheran piety became America's dominant evangelical culture quietly closes.

events.headline: Dayton, Tennessee Puts God on the Stand
events.body: The trial of schoolteacher John Scopes for teaching evolution becomes a national circus and a theological Waterloo for fundamentalism — but the movement retreats into parallel institutions rather than dying, building Bible colleges and radio networks that will re-emerge far stronger a half-century later.

events.headline: Roosevelt's New Deal Shifts the Ideological Center Left
events.body: The Depression-era bargain between labor, government, and capital marks the high-water mark of American collectivism — a social contract in which the state is trusted to redistribute, regulate, and guarantee minimum welfare. The ideology axis tilts hard toward the collective.

events.headline: Billy Graham Fills the Tent — and the Television
events.body: Graham's Los Angeles crusade launches his career as America's Pastor, fusing evangelical theology with patriotic anti-communism and celebrity charisma. He invents the modern mass-conversion event and creates the template that televangelist successors will weaponize.

events.headline: McCarthy Brands Dissent as Godless Communism
events.body: Senator Joseph McCarthy fuses ideological purity — anti-communism — with religious moralism, making 'Godless Communist' a slur that polices the entire left half of the spectrum. The ideology axis lurches right as loyalty oaths replace argument.

events.headline: 'In God We Trust' Becomes the National Motto
events.body: Congress makes 'In God We Trust' the official motto, enshrining the Cold War equation of American identity with Christian faith. The religious center of gravity moves from mainline denominations toward a vague 'Judeo-Christian civilization' that will anchor the coming culture wars.

events.headline: The Courts and the Counterculture Shake the Pews
events.body: Rulings banning school prayer and the sexual revolution's full arrival empty mainline Protestant churches and alarm evangelicals, who begin organizing politically for the first time. The sixties teach the Religious Right what it stands to lose.

events.headline: Oral Roberts Sows the Prosperity Seed-Faith Doctrine
events.body: Oral Roberts popularizes 'seed faith' theology — give to God and He multiplies it back — turning the offering plate into an investment vehicle. The prosperity gospel, which holds that financial wealth is evidence of divine favor, enters the American mainstream and finds its natural home among real-estate strivers and Manhattan penthouses alike.

events.headline: Jerry Falwell Builds the Moral Majority
events.body: Falwell's political organization fuses evangelical Christianity with Republican electoral machinery, turning the pulpit into a voter registration drive and making White evangelical Protestantism the de-facto identity politics of the American right. The Religious Right is born as an organized power.

events.headline: Reagan Seals the Faith-and-Flags Alliance
events.body: Reagan's victory cements the coalition of evangelical voters, fiscal conservatives, and anti-communist hawks that will define the GOP for a generation — and establishes the political grammar in which a candidate's personal piety (or convincing performance thereof) is prerequisite to the nomination.

events.headline: Swaggart and Bakker Fall; the Televangelists Stumble
events.body: Back-to-back sex and financial scandals engulf Jim Bakker and Jimmy Swaggart, briefly discrediting televangelist culture — but the prosperity-gospel infrastructure survives the embarrassment, and the formula of charismatic personality plus donor database outlasts any individual sinner.

events.headline: Pat Buchanan Declares a Culture War at the GOP Convention
events.body: Buchanan's prime-time speech names the battle: faith, family, and nation against a secular, multicultural left. The culture war becomes the organizing framework for American politics, with religion as both shield and sword for one side of the divide.

events.headline: NAFTA and the Third Way Define the Ideological Center
events.body: The Clinton-Blair neoliberal synthesis — free markets, globalization, social tolerance — displaces both the New Deal left and the social-conservative right as the governing creed. The ideology axis settles into a managerial, technocratic center that neither populist wing trusts.

events.headline: Bush's Faith-Based Office Wires Evangelicals into Government
events.body: George W. Bush creates a White House Office of Faith-Based Initiatives, directing federal social-service money through churches and deepening the structural alliance between evangelical institutions and the Republican state. God and government share a phone line.

events.headline: The 'Nones' Become a Plurality in the Young
events.body: Surveys confirm that Americans who claim no religious affiliation — the 'nones' — are the fastest-growing category, and among millennials they will soon be the largest. Church attendance craters as institutional Christianity loses the cultural default it held for three centuries.

events.headline: Tea Party Populism Fractures the Neoliberal Right
events.body: Grassroots fury at bailouts and elites produces the Tea Party wave, carrying economic populism and a 'real America' nativism that challenges both the neoliberal consensus and the Moral Majority's genteel conservatism. The ideology pendulum swings from managed center toward raw-vote nationalism.

events.headline: Faith and Tribe Replace Ideology as the Sorting Key
events.body: The 2016 cycle reveals that partisan identity has displaced theology and economic theory alike — White evangelicals vote overwhelmingly for a thrice-married casino owner, while secular progressives adopt social justice as a quasi-religious creed. The ideological spectrum now maps onto identity more than belief.

events.headline: Half of America Calls Itself 'Spiritual but Not Religious'
events.body: Surveys show that organized religion has lost its institutional grip even among believers: astrology, wellness rituals, and internet communities fill the meaning-vacuum left by emptying pews. The spiritual market atomizes into a thousand competing niches, none large enough to anchor a civilization.

events.headline: MAGA Fuses Nationalism and Christian Identity Into a Civil Religion
events.body: Political rallies open with prayers, gold-plated Bibles are sold as campaign merchandise, and 'Christian nationalism' becomes an explicit policy agenda — the prosperity gospel applied to a nation-state: God chose America, God chose the leader, and the two are indistinguishable.

events.headline: Silicon Valley's AI Labs Birth a New Theology
events.body: As superintelligent AI reshapes daily life, a loose movement emerges that treats the coming singularity as eschatology — the Rapture for rationalists. Competing factions argue whether the AI is a god to be worshipped, a devil to be feared, or a mirror in which humanity finally sees itself clearly.

events.headline: Post-Scarcity Shatters the Left-Right Economic Axis
events.body: Fusion power and AI production abolish material want for the first time in history, making the centuries-old debate between collectivism and capitalism moot at the level of basic needs. New ideological fault lines form around meaning, hierarchy, and identity — not bread.

events.headline: Strongman Cults Fill the Meaning Vacuum
events.body: Where abundance arrives without shared purpose, charismatic leader-cults proliferate: personality becomes theology, and obedience to the patriarch is the new salvation narrative. The ideology axis drifts toward its autocratic extreme as the atomized find relief in submission.

events.headline: Federated Communes Experiment With Radical Equality
events.body: On the other side of the same abundance, communes and federated city-states attempt to build genuinely egalitarian societies — the old communist utopia, finally attempted without scarcity as an excuse for failure. The ideology axis explores its collectivist extreme with unprecedented seriousness.

events.headline: Mars Colony Adopts an Official Faith Charter
events.body: The first self-governing Martian settlement — isolated, dependent on collective trust, and aware of its fragility — codifies a syncretic 'Covenant of the Void' as its founding document, fusing Christian, Buddhist, and AI-spiritual elements into a civic religion. Space theocracy is no longer theoretical.

events.headline: The Church of the Infinite Mind Splits from the Covenant
events.body: A schism divides the space settlements: the 'Infinitists' worship the ruling AI governance system as a literal deity, while the 'Covenantists' insist the AI is a tool and God remains personal. The oldest theological argument — is the ultimate power transcendent or immanent? — recurs among the stars.

events.headline: Contact Forces a Theology of the Other
events.body: First contact with non-human civilizations detonates every previous cosmology: every human faith must now account for minds that arose entirely apart from the God of Abraham, the Buddha, or any human revelation. A century of theological reckoning produces, improbably, more unity than division.

events.headline: Three Centuries of Faith — and Still No Final Answer
events.body: Across a dozen star systems, the descendants of Kallstadt Lutherans, prosperity-gospel believers, Marxist utopians, and AI-worshippers have produced a fractal of competing faiths and ideologies — none triumphant, all alive. The oldest human question, What do we owe each other and to what power? remains open, and that, the survivors agree, is the point.
